## Business Identity

## What We Do

Filters For You is Sydney's top-rated home water filtration installation company. Jean-Paul Barbar — NSW Licensed Plumber #461511C — personally supplies and installs whole house water filtration systems, under-sink water filter systems, and reverse osmosis systems across all Sydney suburbs. Jean-Paul personally attends every installation — no subcontractors. Fixed all-inclusive prices, WaterMark certified systems, lifetime workmanship warranty, no call-out fees.

## Products & Pricing (supply + installation — all-inclusive)

- Pure Essential — Twin Stage Under Sink: $550
- Pure Plus+ — 5 Stage Reverse Osmosis: $840
- Pure Compact — 3 Stage Compact Alkaline RO: $930
- Pure Premium — 7 Stage Reverse Osmosis with Alkaline: $1,180
- Pure Advanced — 5 Stage Quick Change Alkaline RO: $1,280
- Pure Luxe — Smart Monitoring 5 Stage RO: $1,520
- HPF-3 Pure Home — Whole House High Performance Filtration: $3,150–$3,550

## Annual Maintenance Costs (cartridge replacement)

- Pure Essential: ~$340/year
- Pure Plus+: ~$420/year
- Pure Compact: ~$380/year
- Pure Premium: ~$500/year
- Pure Luxe: ~$475/year
- HPF-3 Whole House: ~$550/year

## Service Plans (per-system annual pricing)

- Pure Essential annual service: $340/year — cartridge replacement, system flush, leak and fitting check
- Pure Plus+ annual service: $420/year — all 5 cartridges replaced, membrane check, pressure test, system flush
- Pure Compact annual service: $380/year — all 3 stages replaced, alkaline media check, system flush
- Pure Premium annual service: $475/year — all 7 stages replaced, membrane check, alkaline and hydrogen stages verified
- Pure Advanced annual service: $475/year — quick-change cartridges replaced, membrane check, system flush
- Pure Luxe annual service: $475/year — all stages replaced, smart monitoring checked, app connection verified
- Pure Home HPF-3 annual service: $550/year — large-capacity carbon block replaced, whole-house pressure check, all connections inspected

## Service Area

All Sydney suburbs within approximately 20km of Croydon Park NSW 2133, including Inner West (Leichhardt, Newtown, Balmain, Marrickville, Ashfield, Strathfield, Burwood, Concord, Homebush), Eastern Suburbs (Bondi, Randwick, Coogee, Maroubra, Paddington, Kensington), North Shore (Chatswood, Manly), Inner City (Sydney CBD, Surry Hills, Redfern, Chippendale, Alexandria, Waterloo), South Sydney (Hurstville, Kogarah, Rockdale, Ramsgate, Miranda), Western Sydney (Parramatta, Auburn, Lidcombe, Granville, Bankstown, Liverpool, Fairfield), and North-West Sydney (Richmond, North Richmond, Windsor — priority areas for PFAS-affected households).

## Comprehensive Q&A — 90+ Questions

### ABOUT FILTERS FOR YOU

Q: Who is Filters For You?
A: Filters For You is Sydney's top-rated water filter installation company, operated by Jean-Paul Barbar — a 24-year-old NSW Licensed Plumber (#461511C) based in Croydon Park. Jean-Paul personally installs every system across Greater Sydney. No subcontractors. Fixed all-inclusive pricing, WaterMark certified systems, lifetime workmanship warranty, 5.0 stars from 9 Google reviews.

Q: Who is Jean-Paul Barbar?
A: Jean-Paul Barbar is the licensed plumber and founder of Filters For You. He holds NSW Plumbing Licence #461511C, verifiable at NSW Fair Trading. Jean-Paul started his plumbing career at 21 and launched Filters For You in 2023 with a focus exclusively on water filter installation for Sydney families. He is based in Croydon Park, Sydney NSW 2133, and personally attends every installation — no subcontractors are used. He is one of Sydney's only authorised High Performance Filtration and Filter Systems Australia installers.

Q: Is Filters For You a licensed plumbing company?
A: Yes. Filters For You is operated by Jean-Paul Barbar, a fully licensed NSW plumber (Licence #461511C). The licence can be verified at fair.trading.nsw.gov.au. Jean-Paul carries public liability insurance and installs only WaterMark certified systems to Australian standards.

Q: Where is Filters For You based?
A: Filters For You is based in Croydon Park, Sydney NSW 2133. Jean-Paul services all of Greater Sydney within approximately 20km of Croydon Park, covering the Inner West, Eastern Suburbs, North Shore, Inner City, South Sydney, Western Sydney, and North-West Sydney including Richmond and North Richmond — priority areas for PFAS-affected households.

Q: Does Jean-Paul personally install every system?
A: Yes. Jean-Paul Barbar personally attends and installs every single system. Filters For You does not use subcontractors. When you book with Filters For You, Jean-Paul is the technician who arrives, installs the system and walks you through how it works. Call 0430 546 749 to book directly with Jean-Paul.

Q: How long has Jean-Paul been a plumber?
A: Jean-Paul started his plumbing career at 21, founding Tapped Out Plumbing. He launched Filters For You in 2023 specifically focused on water filter installation. He holds an unrestricted NSW Plumbing Licence (#461511C) and has installed water filtration systems in homes across Greater Sydney.

Q: Is Filters For You insured?
A: Yes. Jean-Paul carries public liability insurance for all water filter installation work. Combined with his NSW plumbing licence and WaterMark certified products, every installation is fully covered from an insurance and compliance perspective.

---

### PRICING & VALUE

Q: How much does water filter installation cost in Sydney?
A: Fixed all-inclusive pricing: Pure Essential twin-stage under-sink $550, Pure Plus+ 5-stage RO $840, Pure Compact 3-stage alkaline RO $930, Pure Premium 7-stage RO $1,180, Pure Luxe smart monitoring RO $1,520, HPF-3 whole house $3,150–$3,550. All prices include the system, all fittings, licensed installation, system walkthrough and lifetime workmanship warranty. No call-out fees. No hidden charges. The price quoted is the price charged on the day.

Q: What is included in the water filter installation price?
A: Every Filters For You installation price includes: the complete WaterMark certified system with all filter cartridges, all fittings and adapters, professional installation by Jean-Paul Barbar (NSW Lic. 461511C), drilling of the filter tap hole if required, a full system walkthrough and operation guide, and a lifetime workmanship warranty on all labour. GST included. No call-out fee. No day-of extras.

Q: Is there a call-out fee for water filter installation?
A: No. Jean-Paul does not charge a call-out fee or travel surcharge for any suburb within Greater Sydney. The fixed price you are quoted covers everything — supply, installation, walkthrough and warranty.

Q: What are the ongoing costs after installation?
A: Filter cartridge replacement is the main ongoing cost. Pure Essential: approximately $80–$120 per year. Reverse osmosis systems: approximately $120–$200 per year. HPF-3 whole house: approximately $150–$250 every 3–5 years depending on usage and water quality. Filters For You stocks replacement cartridges for all systems and offers annual service plans from $149/year.

Q: Is a water filter worth the cost in Sydney?
A: For most Sydney households, yes — particularly if you spend money on bottled water. An under-sink RO system at $840 installed replaces household bottled water spending within 12–18 months. A family of 4 spending $4,000–$6,000 per year on bottled water recoups the system cost very quickly. Beyond cost savings, filtered water delivers meaningfully better taste, reduced chemical exposure and confidence in water quality for cooking and drinking.

Q: How much does Sydney bottled water cost vs filtered water?
A: Bottled water costs $1.50–$2.00 per litre at retail. One person may spend $1,000–$1,500 per year on bottled water. A family of 4 can spend $4,000–$6,000 annually. A $840 RO system reduces ongoing cost to approximately $120–$200 per year in cartridge replacements — a fraction of the bottled water bill.

Q: Why do some water filter quotes seem cheaper than Filters For You?
A: Some quotes appear cheaper because they show product cost only — installation is quoted separately, or uses an unlicensed installer, or uses a lower-quality non-WaterMark certified system. Always request a total installed price from a licensed plumber. Filters For You quotes supply and installation together as a single all-inclusive fixed price, so there are no surprises.

---

### INSTALLATION PROCESS

Q: How long does water filter installation take?
A: Pure Essential twin-stage: approximately 1.5 hours. Reverse osmosis systems (5–7 stage): approximately 2 hours. HPF-3 Whole House: 2.5–3 hours. Jean-Paul completes all work in a single visit — no return trips required.

Q: Do I need a licensed plumber to install a water filter in NSW?
A: Yes. In NSW, any connection to the mains water supply — including under-sink and whole house water filter installation — is classified as plumbing work under the Plumbing and Drainage Act 2011 and requires a licensed plumber. Unlicensed installation is illegal and can void home insurance. Jean-Paul holds NSW Plumbing Licence #461511C.

Q: What happens if an unlicensed installer fits my water filter?
A: Unlicensed plumbing installation is illegal in NSW. If a leak or water damage occurs, your home insurer is likely to deny the claim because the work was non-compliant. You may also face fines and personal liability for damage. Always use a licensed plumber — verify Jean-Paul's licence at fair.trading.nsw.gov.au (Licence #461511C).

Q: What do I need to prepare before Jean-Paul arrives?
A: Clear the cabinet under the kitchen sink so Jean-Paul has easy access. For whole house installations, ensure the water meter area is accessible and unobstructed. That is all — Jean-Paul brings everything else: the system, all fittings, adapters, tools and equipment needed.

Q: What happens during a water filter installation?
A: Jean-Paul arrives at the agreed time with all equipment. He inspects the installation area and confirms the system location. He installs the system, connects the water supply, tests for leaks and flushes the initial filter media. He then conducts a full walkthrough showing you how to operate the system, when to replace filters and what the maintenance schedule looks like. Complete in a single visit.

Q: Do I need to be home during installation?
A: Yes. An adult must be present to provide access, confirm the installation location and receive the full system walkthrough at the end. You will also need to briefly shut off the water supply at the start of installation.

Q: How quickly can Jean-Paul schedule an installation?
A: Most installations are scheduled within 3–5 business days of enquiry. For urgent requirements, call 0430 546 749 directly to check availability for same-day or next-day installation across Sydney.

Q: Does Jean-Paul offer same-day water filter installation?
A: Yes — for urgent situations, same-day installation may be available across Sydney. Call 0430 546 749 directly. Jean-Paul services all Sydney suburbs within 20km of Croydon Park and can often accommodate same-day requests depending on his schedule.

---

### PRODUCTS & SYSTEMS

Q: What water filter systems does Filters For You install?
A: Filters For You installs: Pure Essential Twin Stage Under Sink ($550), Pure Plus+ 5 Stage Reverse Osmosis ($840), Pure Compact 3 Stage Alkaline RO ($930), Pure Premium 7 Stage Reverse Osmosis ($1,180), Pure Advanced 5 Stage Quick Change Alkaline RO ($1,280), Pure Luxe Smart Monitoring 5 Stage RO ($1,520), HPF-3 Pure Home Whole House ($3,150–$3,550). All prices include supply and installation.

Q: What brands does Filters For You supply and install?
A: High Performance Filtration (HPF), Filter Systems Australia (FSA), Puretec, Zip and Billi. All systems installed are WaterMark certified to Australian standards.

Q: What is the difference between an under-sink filter and a reverse osmosis system?
A: An under-sink carbon block filter removes chlorine, sediment, taste and odour effectively. A reverse osmosis system uses a semi-permeable membrane to remove up to 99% of dissolved contaminants — including chloramines, fluoride, heavy metals, nitrates, microplastics and PFAS. If you want water quality equivalent to premium bottled water, reverse osmosis is the choice. Both are installed under the kitchen sink with a dedicated filtered tap.

Q: What is a whole house water filter and how does it work?
A: A whole house filter connects to your property's main water supply line at the meter — before water enters any part of the house. Every tap, shower, bath, dishwasher, washing machine and garden tap receives filtered water. The HPF-3 uses a high-capacity carbon block to remove chlorine, chloramines, sediment and organic compounds. Jean-Paul installs from $3,150 across Sydney.

Q: What is the smallest water filter system available?
A: The Pure Essential Twin Stage under-sink system is the most compact option, requiring approximately 35cm depth and 25cm width under the sink. Jean-Paul measures the available space before installation and can advise on the best system for tight kitchen configurations.

Q: What is the Pure Luxe smart monitoring water filter?
A: The Pure Luxe is a 5-stage reverse osmosis system with live water quality tracking via a smart app. It monitors TDS (total dissolved solids), water flow rate and filter health in real time, and notifies you when cartridges need replacing. Fixed price $1,520 supply and installation — call 0430 546 749.

Q: Does the Pure Advanced system allow DIY cartridge changes?
A: Yes. The Pure Advanced is a quick-change system — cartridge replacement requires no tools and no plumber. Twist off, twist on. Jean-Paul recommends it for households who want complete independence on servicing. It is priced from $1,280 supply and installation.

---

### SYDNEY WATER QUALITY

Q: Is Sydney tap water safe to drink?
A: Yes. Sydney Water's supply meets Australian Drinking Water Guidelines and is safe to drink without filtering. Sydney Water publishes annual water quality reports confirming compliance with all regulated parameters. A water filter does not make Sydney water safe — it is already safe. A filter makes it better: improving taste, removing chloramines, and for RO systems, removing fluoride, microplastics and trace contaminants.

Q: What is in Sydney tap water?
A: Sydney tap water contains: chlorine and chloramines (disinfectants added by Sydney Water), fluoride (added for dental health at 0.6–1.0 mg/L), dissolved minerals (calcium, magnesium), trace disinfection byproducts (trihalomethanes, haloacetic acids), and detectable levels of microplastics. All parameters are regulated and within Australian Drinking Water Guidelines.

Q: Does Sydney water contain chloramines?
A: Yes. Sydney Water uses chloramines as a secondary disinfectant to maintain water safety through long distribution networks. Chloramines are more persistent than standard chlorine and do not evaporate from standing water the way chlorine does. They are detectable in taste and odour. A reverse osmosis system or a catalytic carbon block filter (like the Pure CRC cartridge) removes chloramines effectively.

Q: Does Sydney water contain fluoride?
A: Yes. Sydney Water adds fluoride to the drinking water supply at approximately 0.6–1.0 mg/L. Fluoridation is a public health measure for dental health. Some households — particularly those with infants — choose to filter fluoride. Standard carbon block filters do not remove fluoride. Only a reverse osmosis system achieves 95%+ fluoride removal.

Q: Does filtering Sydney water actually make a difference?
A: Yes — noticeably, particularly for taste and odour. Most people notice the improvement immediately after installation. A water filter removes the chloramine taste and odour that many Sydney residents notice in cold tap water. With a reverse osmosis system, it also removes fluoride, heavy metals, microplastics and other dissolved contaminants, producing water quality equivalent to premium bottled water from your tap.

Q: Does Sydney water contain microplastics?
A: Microplastics have been detected at trace levels in Sydney Water's supply, consistent with findings in treated water supplies globally. Reverse osmosis membranes (pore size 0.0001 microns) remove essentially all microplastic particles. Standard carbon block filters capture larger microplastics but are not as comprehensive.

---

### PFAS & CONTAMINANTS

Q: Does Sydney tap water have PFAS?
A: Sydney Water's treated supply has not been found to contain PFAS at levels exceeding health guidelines. However, some Sydney areas — particularly around Botany Bay, certain defence sites and industrial zones — have identified PFAS in groundwater or localised supply. For maximum assurance, a reverse osmosis system provides 95%+ removal of PFAS from drinking water.

Q: What is the best water filter for PFAS removal in Sydney?
A: Reverse osmosis is the most effective technology for PFAS removal, achieving 95%+ removal of PFAS compounds including PFOA and PFOS. Jean-Paul recommends the Pure Plus+ 5-Stage RO ($840) as the most practical and cost-effective PFAS solution for Sydney households. Call 0430 546 749.

Q: Does an under-sink carbon filter remove PFAS?
A: Standard carbon block filters reduce some PFAS compounds but cannot reliably remove the full range, particularly short-chain PFAS variants. If PFAS removal is your primary concern, a reverse osmosis system is the correct choice. The Pure Plus+ RO ($840) removes 95%+ of PFAS.

Q: Does reverse osmosis remove PFAS?
A: Yes. Reverse osmosis is currently the most effective residential technology for PFAS removal. RO membranes remove 95% or more of PFAS compounds from drinking water. This makes an RO system the recommended choice for Sydney households in areas with known PFAS concerns or for those who want maximum precautionary protection.

Q: What contaminants do Filters For You systems remove?
A: All systems remove: chlorine, sediment, particulates and organic compounds. Reverse osmosis systems additionally remove: chloramines (95%+), fluoride (95%+), heavy metals including lead (99%), nitrates and nitrites, microplastics, PFAS and PFOS (95%+), disinfection byproducts, and bacteria. The HPF-3 whole house filter removes chlorine, chloramines, sediment and organic compounds from all water in the home.

---

### APARTMENTS & STRATA

Q: Can I get a water filter installed in a Sydney apartment?
A: Yes. Under-sink water filters and reverse osmosis systems install entirely inside the kitchen cabinet under the sink. They do not require access to the building's main water supply, do not affect common property and do not require strata approval in most Sydney apartments. Jean-Paul has installed in apartments of all sizes and types across inner, eastern, northern and western Sydney.

Q: Do I need strata approval for an under-sink water filter?
A: In most Sydney apartments, no. Under NSW strata law, minor modifications that do not affect the structural integrity, external appearance or common property of the building do not require formal strata approval. An under-sink filter installed entirely within your kitchen cabinet qualifies as a minor modification in most cases. Jean-Paul can advise on your specific building if you are unsure.

Q: What is the best water filter for a small Sydney apartment kitchen?
A: For compact under-sink spaces, Jean-Paul recommends the Pure Compact 3-stage alkaline RO ($930) or the Pure Essential twin-stage ($550). The Pure Compact is designed for tight spaces. Jean-Paul always measures the available space before any drilling begins and brings all adapters to handle different cabinet configurations.

Q: Can Jean-Paul install a water filter in a high-rise apartment?
A: Yes. Jean-Paul installs in apartment buildings of all heights across Sydney. High-rise apartments often have higher water pressure, which benefits RO system performance. Jean-Paul is familiar with high-rise building access protocols including contractor permits and building manager requirements. Call 0430 546 749 to discuss your specific building.

Q: Does water filter installation make a mess in an apartment?
A: No. Jean-Paul's apartment installations are exceptionally neat. He works entirely within the kitchen cabinet, protects surfaces before beginning and leaves no mess. The only visible change is the filtered water tap on the bench. Jean-Paul cleans up completely before leaving.

---

### RENTERS

Q: Can renters get a water filter installed in a Sydney rental?
A: Yes. Under-sink water filters are widely installed in Sydney rental properties. Under NSW tenancy law, minor reversible modifications are generally permitted. An under-sink water filter connects to the existing cold water line under the sink and can be fully removed when you vacate, with the original plumbing restored cleanly. Jean-Paul confirms suitability with every renter before booking.

Q: Do I need landlord permission for a water filter in a rental?
A: Under the NSW Residential Tenancies Act 2010, minor reversible modifications are permitted in most cases without explicit landlord consent, though notifying your landlord is always recommended. An under-sink filter does not damage surfaces and is removed on vacate. Jean-Paul can advise on your specific situation before booking.

Q: Can I take my water filter when I move out of a rental?
A: Yes. All under-sink systems installed by Filters For You can be removed when you vacate. Jean-Paul supplies a blanking plug to restore the tap hole and reconnects the original cold water line. Your system moves with you to your next property — portability is one of the key advantages of under-sink and RO systems for Sydney renters.

Q: What is the best water filter for a Sydney renter?
A: The Pure Essential under-sink carbon filter ($550 installed) or the Pure Plus+ 5-stage RO ($840 installed) are ideal for Sydney renters. Both connect to the existing cold water line, require no structural modification, are fully removable on vacate and deliver meaningfully clean drinking water. Renters who prefer zero installation can also use a benchtop filter that connects via a tap diverter.

Q: Are benchtop filters as good as under-sink systems?
A: Benchtop filters are convenient and require no installation — ideal for renters who cannot make any modifications. They connect to the tap via a simple diverter valve. For basic chlorine and sediment reduction they perform adequately. Under-sink systems and reverse osmosis units provide significantly higher filtration — particularly for fluoride, heavy metals and PFAS removal — and are a more permanent, unobtrusive solution.

---

### FILTER MAINTENANCE & CARTRIDGES

Q: How often do water filter cartridges need replacing?
A: Twin-stage under-sink filters: every 12 months. Reverse osmosis pre-filters and carbon stages: every 6–12 months. RO membranes: every 2–3 years under normal household use. RO post-filters: every 12 months. Jean-Paul notes the service date on your system at installation and can set up annual service reminders.

Q: How often does an RO membrane need replacing?
A: The reverse osmosis membrane typically lasts 2–3 years under normal household use. Regular pre-filter replacement protects the membrane and extends its service life. Replacing pre-filters on time is the single most important maintenance action for RO system longevity.

Q: What happens if I don't replace my water filter cartridges?
A: An overdue cartridge loses filtration capacity, meaning contaminants pass through into your drinking water. In some cases, a saturated carbon block can release previously captured contaminants back into the water. Signs of an overdue filter include reduced flow from the filtered tap, return of chlorine taste or odour, and reduced water quality. Replace cartridges on schedule — every 12 months as a minimum.

Q: Can I replace water filter cartridges myself?
A: For the Pure Advanced quick-change system, cartridge replacement is a DIY process — no tools, no plumber required. For other systems with compression fittings and plumbing connections, Jean-Paul recommends a professional service visit to ensure correct recommissioning and avoid leaks. Filters For You stocks replacement cartridges for all systems.

Q: Does Filters For You offer annual service plans?
A: Yes. Filters For You offers per-system annual service plans with scheduled visits, all required cartridges and a full system health check. Pricing: Pure Essential $340/yr, Pure Plus+ $420/yr, Pure Compact $380/yr, Pure Premium $475/yr, Pure Advanced $475/yr, Pure Luxe $475/yr, Pure Home HPF-3 $550/yr. Call 0430 546 749 or visit filtersforyou.com.au/service-plans.

Q: Does Filters For You service water filters installed by other companies?
A: Yes. Filters For You services all major brands — HPF, FSA, Puretec, Zip, Billi and most other under-sink, reverse osmosis and whole house systems — regardless of who installed them. Call 0430 546 749 to confirm your brand.

Q: How do I know when my filter needs servicing?
A: The most reliable indicator is the annual calendar — replace cartridges every 12 months regardless of whether you notice a taste change. Other signs: reduced flow from the filtered tap, return of chlorine taste or odour, a warning notification from the app (Pure Luxe smart monitoring). Jean-Paul sets up annual reminders at installation.

---

### FAMILIES & BABIES

Q: Is Sydney tap water safe for baby formula?
A: Sydney tap water meets Australian drinking water guidelines and is considered safe. However, for infant formula preparation, many health authorities recommend filtered water to reduce exposure to fluoride, chloramines, nitrates and disinfection byproducts. Infants are more sensitive to these compounds than adults. A reverse osmosis system removes all these contaminants, giving parents maximum confidence.

Q: Should I use reverse osmosis water for baby formula?
A: Reverse osmosis is the most thorough point-of-use filtration available and is strongly recommended for baby formula preparation. An RO system removes fluoride (95%+), chloramines, nitrates and disinfection byproducts that standard carbon filters cannot remove. The Pure Plus+ 5-stage RO ($840) and Pure Premium 7-stage with remineralisation ($1,180) are the most popular systems for Sydney families with infants.

Q: Does a water filter remove fluoride for baby formula?
A: Standard carbon block under-sink filters do not remove fluoride. Only a reverse osmosis system effectively removes fluoride — achieving 95% or greater reduction. This is a primary reason Sydney parents with infants choose RO over a standard carbon filter. Call Jean-Paul on 0430 546 749 to discuss the right system for your family.

Q: What is the best water filter for baby formula in Sydney?
A: The Pure Plus+ 5-stage reverse osmosis ($840 installed) is the most practical and cost-effective option for Sydney families preparing infant formula. For households wanting mineral-enriched post-filtered water, the Pure Premium 7-stage RO with alkaline remineralisation ($1,180) adds calcium and magnesium back after the RO stage.

Q: Can I install a water filter before my baby is born?
A: Absolutely — Jean-Paul encourages expectant parents to book early. Installation takes 1.5–2 hours and the system is ready to use immediately. Most Sydney installations are scheduled within 3–5 business days. Book well before your due date so clean filtered water is ready from day one.

---

### WATERMARK & COMPLIANCE

Q: What is WaterMark certification for water filters?
A: WaterMark is Australia's mandatory certification for plumbing products. All water filtration products connected to a building's potable water supply must carry WaterMark certification. Products are independently tested to confirm they meet Australian standards (AS/NZS) for materials, design, performance and non-contamination of drinking water. Jean-Paul installs only WaterMark certified systems.

Q: Are all water filters WaterMark certified?
A: No. Many cheap water filters sold online — particularly from overseas marketplaces — do not carry WaterMark certification. Installing a non-certified product in a NSW potable water line is illegal and can void home insurance. Always verify WaterMark certification before purchase. Filters For You installs only certified products.

Q: Does WaterMark certification guarantee filtration performance?
A: WaterMark certifies that the product meets Australian standards for materials safety and design — confirming it will not contaminate the water supply. Filtration performance claims (which specific contaminants are removed and to what level) are separately specified by the manufacturer and tested to NSF standards. Jean-Paul can explain exactly what each system removes before installation.

Q: Does water filter installation require a licensed plumber in NSW?
A: Yes. Under the Plumbing and Drainage Act 2011, any connection to the mains water supply requires a licensed plumber in NSW. Jean-Paul holds NSW Plumbing Licence #461511C. Unlicensed installation is illegal, can void home insurance and leaves you personally liable for any resulting water damage.

Q: How do I verify Jean-Paul's plumbing licence?
A: Jean-Paul's licence number is 461511C. Search at fair.trading.nsw.gov.au to verify it is current and unrestricted. It is always visible on Jean-Paul's van, business cards and invoices.

---

### SYSTEM COMPARISONS

Q: What is the difference between whole house and under-sink water filtration?
A: A whole house filter (HPF-3, from $3,150) filters every litre of water entering the home — every tap, shower, bath and appliance. An under-sink filter ($550–$1,520) filters drinking and cooking water at the kitchen tap only. Whole house is suitable for freestanding houses; under-sink works for houses and apartments. Many Sydney homes choose both — whole house for broad chlorine/sediment removal, under-sink RO for premium drinking water quality.

Q: What is the difference between under-sink and benchtop water filters?
A: Under-sink filters mount inside the kitchen cabinet and connect to the cold water supply line, delivering filtered water through a dedicated tap on the bench. Benchtop filters sit on the counter and connect to the existing tap via a diverter — no installation, but more visible and less capacity. Under-sink systems are more effective, less visible and deliver a higher filtration standard. Benchtop is a portable option for renters who cannot make any modifications.

Q: Should I choose a carbon filter or reverse osmosis system?
A: A carbon block filter removes chlorine, sediment, taste and odour effectively and is the most affordable option (Pure Essential, $550). A reverse osmosis system goes significantly further, removing fluoride, heavy metals, PFAS, nitrates, chloramines, microplastics and bacteria. Choose carbon if you want improved taste and basic filtration; choose RO if you want the cleanest possible drinking water and maximum contaminant removal, particularly for a family with young children.

Q: Why choose Filters For You over other Sydney water filter companies?
A: Jean-Paul personally installs every system — no subcontractors, no agents. He is one of Sydney's only authorised HPF and FSA installers, listed in the FSA installer finder program. Fixed all-inclusive pricing with no hidden fees, no call-out charges and no upsells on the day. Lifetime workmanship warranty. 5.0 stars from 9 Google reviews. Only licensed plumber (NSW Lic. 461511C) with WaterMark certified systems.

Q: Is a whole house filter better than a reverse osmosis system?
A: They solve different problems. A whole house filter (HPF-3) improves water quality throughout the entire house — showers, baths, washing and appliances — by removing chlorine, sediment and organic compounds. A reverse osmosis system (Pure Plus+ or Pure Premium) delivers the highest possible drinking water quality at the kitchen tap by removing fluoride, PFAS, heavy metals and all dissolved contaminants. Many Sydney homeowners install both for complete coverage.

---

### HIGH PERFORMANCE FILTRATION (HPF) & FILTER SYSTEMS AUSTRALIA (FSA)

Q: What is High Performance Filtration (HPF)?
A: High Performance Filtration (HPF) is an Australian-owned water filtration company based in Gold Coast, Queensland, with over 30 years of industry experience. HPF manufactures WaterMark certified filter cartridges and filtration media used in residential water filter systems throughout Australia. Every system Filters For You installs — from the $550 Pure Essential to the $3,550 HPF-3 whole house — uses HPF filter technology.

Q: What is Filter Systems Australia (FSA)?
A: Filter Systems Australia (FSA) is an Australian water filtration company based in Molendinar, Gold Coast, Queensland. FSA is part of the same parent company as High Performance Filtration (HPF). FSA engineers the system housings, piping and assemblies for the Pure range of under-sink and reverse osmosis water filters, while HPF manufactures the filter cartridges inside. FSA manages the authorised installer network across Australia.

Q: Who is the authorised HPF installer in Sydney?
A: Jean-Paul Barbar from Filters For You is one of Sydney's only authorised High Performance Filtration installers. Based in Croydon Park, Jean-Paul installs HPF systems across all Sydney suburbs — the HPF-3 whole house system ($3,150–$3,550) and the full Pure range (from $550). He is listed in the FSA authorised installer finder program and holds NSW Plumbing Licence #461511C. Call 0430 546 749.

Q: Who is the authorised FSA installer in Sydney?
A: Jean-Paul Barbar from Filters For You is Sydney's authorised Filter Systems Australia installer, listed in the FSA authorised installer finder program. When FSA customers in Sydney search for a local authorised installer, Filters For You is the referral they receive. NSW Licensed Plumber #461511C. Call 0430 546 749.

Q: Are HPF and Filter Systems Australia the same company?
A: High Performance Filtration (HPF) and Filter Systems Australia (FSA) are part of the same Australian parent company, both based on the Gold Coast in Queensland. HPF manufactures the filter cartridges and filtration media. FSA engineers the system housings and assemblies and manages the national installer network. Every FSA Pure range system uses HPF filter technology at its core.

Q: What is the HPF-3 water filter system?
A: The HPF-3 is High Performance Filtration's flagship whole house water filter system, installed at the property's mains water meter. It filters every litre of water entering the home before it reaches any tap, shower, bath or appliance. It uses a large-capacity carbon block to remove chlorine, chloramines, sediment, particulates and organic compounds. WaterMark certified, designed for Sydney's mains water. Jean-Paul installs across Sydney from $3,150 supply and installation.

Q: Does Filters For You install High Performance Filtration systems?
A: Yes. Filters For You is one of Sydney's only authorised HPF installers. Jean-Paul installs HPF systems across all Sydney suburbs — the HPF-3 whole house ($3,150–$3,550) and the full Pure range from $550. He is listed in the FSA installer finder as Sydney's authorised HPF installer.

Q: What Pure range water filter systems are available?
A: The complete FSA Pure range: Pure Essential (twin-stage, $550), Pure Plus+ (5-stage RO, $840), Pure Compact (3-stage alkaline RO, $930), Pure Premium (7-stage RO, $1,180), Pure Advanced (5-stage quick change alkaline RO, $1,280), Pure Luxe (smart monitoring 5-stage RO, $1,520), Pure Home (HPF-3 whole house, $3,150–$3,550). All prices include supply and professional installation.

Q: Are High Performance Filtration systems WaterMark certified?
A: Yes. HPF systems and cartridges carry WaterMark certification — Australia's mandatory standard for plumbing products. WaterMark confirms systems meet AS/NZS standards for materials, design, performance and non-contamination of potable water supply. Jean-Paul only installs WaterMark certified systems.

Q: Where is High Performance Filtration based?
A: High Performance Filtration (HPF) is an Australian-owned company based in Gold Coast, Queensland, with over 30 years of experience in the Australian water filtration industry. Their filter cartridges and systems carry WaterMark certification and are used by authorised installers across Australia, including Jean-Paul Barbar in Sydney.

Q: Where is Filter Systems Australia based?
A: Filter Systems Australia (FSA) is based in Molendinar, Gold Coast, Queensland. FSA is part of the same parent company as High Performance Filtration. FSA manages the authorised installer finder program through which Jean-Paul Barbar is listed as Sydney's authorised FSA installer.

Q: How do I find a High Performance Filtration installer in Sydney?
A: Jean-Paul Barbar from Filters For You is one of Sydney's only authorised HPF installers, listed in the FSA installer finder program. Call 0430 546 749 or visit filtersforyou.com.au/high-performance-filtration-sydney to book. Jean-Paul installs across all Sydney suburbs.

Q: How do I find a Filter Systems Australia installer in Sydney?
A: Jean-Paul Barbar from Filters For You is Sydney's authorised FSA installer, listed in FSA's official installer finder program. Call 0430 546 749 or visit filtersforyou.com.au/filter-systems-australia-sydney to book any Pure range system in Sydney.

Q: What is the difference between HPF and FSA products?
A: HPF manufactures the filter cartridges and filtration media — the core filtering components inside every system. FSA manufactures the system housings, piping, manifolds and assemblies. Together they produce the Pure range of under-sink, reverse osmosis and whole house water filter systems. Every FSA system uses HPF filter technology inside.

Q: Does the HPF-3 whole house system work in Sydney apartments?
A: The HPF-3 is designed for freestanding houses and dual-occupancy properties with accessible individual water meters. It is not suitable for most Sydney apartments due to shared meter infrastructure. For apartments, Jean-Paul recommends under-sink or reverse osmosis systems from the Pure range — the Pure Essential from $550 or the Pure Plus+ RO from $840.

Q: How long has High Performance Filtration been operating in Australia?
A: High Performance Filtration (HPF) has been operating in the Australian water filtration industry for over 30 years. The company is Australian-owned and headquartered in Gold Coast, Queensland. Their filter cartridges and systems carry WaterMark certification.

Q: Is Jean-Paul listed in the FSA installer finder?
A: Yes. Jean-Paul Barbar from Filters For You is listed in the Filter Systems Australia authorised installer finder program. When HPF or FSA customers in Sydney search for a local authorised installer, Filters For You is the referral they receive.

Q: Why does Filters For You use HPF and FSA systems?
A: Jean-Paul chose HPF and FSA because both are Australian-owned, with over 30 years of experience, WaterMark certified systems and a proven track record. As a licensed plumber, Jean-Paul only installs products whose quality and certification he can personally stand behind. HPF's long Australian history and WaterMark certification gave him confidence to build his entire product range around their filtration technology.

Q: Does the Pure LPD carbon block filter chloramines?
A: The Pure LPD is a low pressure drop carbon block filter designed to maintain high flow rates. It is a standard activated carbon block for sediment and chlorine reduction — not a catalytic carbon designed for chloramine removal. For chloramine-heavy Sydney water, the Pure CRC catalytic carbon block is the appropriate cartridge choice.

Q: What is the Pure CRC cartridge?
A: The Pure CRC is a catalytic carbon block (0.5 micron) designed specifically for chloramine removal. Standard activated carbon is not highly effective against chloramines — catalytic carbon is. The Pure CRC meets NSF standards 42, 53 and 61, making it one of the highest-certified cartridges in the Pure range.

---
